pattern: Search the BST for the target key, then restructure around the deleted node using the inorder successor to maintain BST order.
hints: Recursively navigate left or right based on key comparison until the node to remove is found; when not found, return the subtree unchanged.
When the node to delete has only one child (or none), the answer is simple — return that child directly.
When the node has two children, find the smallest node in the right subtree (the inorder successor), copy its value up to the current node, then delete that successor from the right subtree.

edge_inputs.description: delete the root of a three-node tree
delete node with only a left child
delete node with only a right child
delete node with two children where successor has its own right child
delete root when it has two children

stress.description: randomly built BST of up to 200 nodes, delete a randomly chosen present key
stress.note: key is always present so deletion always fires
